prompt:
hey — docs folks keep hand-editing the tables of contents in our markdown and they are permanently stale. can you build us a small TOC tool? plain node/TypeScript, zero npm deps.

what I need:
- toc.ts exporting extractHeadings(markdown), slugify(text), generateToc(markdown, opts?), insertToc(markdown, opts?)
- cli.ts so CI can run it: `node cli.ts <file>` rewrites the file in place between `<!-- toc -->` / `<!-- tocstop -->` marker lines and prints `updated <file>` (or `unchanged <file>` when it's already current). `--check <file>` never writes: exit 1 + print `stale <file>` if a rewrite would change it, exit 0 + `ok <file>` otherwise. `--max-level N` caps heading depth. file has no markers → leave it alone, print `no toc markers in <file>` to stderr, exit 2. no args or unreadable file → stderr (usage / the filename) and exit 2.

heading rules (github-style anchors):
- ATX only: 1-6 `#` at line start followed by a space. `#nospace` and 7+ hashes don't count. ignore anything inside ``` or ~~~ fences.
- `## Title ##` closing hashes get stripped. inline markdown flattens to its text: `code` ticks and **bold**/*em* stars drop, [links](url) keep just the link text.
- slug: lowercase, keep letters/digits/spaces/hyphens/underscores, drop everything else, THEN each space becomes a hyphen — so 'Install & Run' → install--run (double hyphen, yes, that's what github does).
- duplicate slugs get -1, -2, ... suffixes in document order, and the counter spans ALL heading levels — a level-4 'Notes' still claims #notes even when the TOC omits level 4, so a later level-2 'Notes' links to #notes-1.

TOC shape: `- [Text](#slug)` lines, two spaces of indent per level below the shallowest included heading, include levels up to maxLevel (default 3). insertToc replaces whatever currently sits between the markers with blank line / list / blank line, so running it twice is a no-op; no markers → return the input string unchanged.

fixtures/guide.md is a sample doc to eyeball. the acceptance tests are the contract — run `node --test test_toc.ts test_cli.ts` (don't edit those two). structure beyond the two entry points is yours; I'd keep the slug/heading scanning separate from the file-rewriting bits.
